unit.note: F0 fresh gate green. Server vitest 168 files / 1939 passed + 1 skipped (1940) (SHIPWRIGHT_NETWORK_PROFILE=local) + npm run build green. Client ALSO re-run (client label-logic touched): 204 files / 1983 passed + npm run build (tsc+vite) green. New D18 coverage: server resume-on-jsonl.test.ts (5 tests through the REAL SessionWatcher + REAL on-disk <uuid>.jsonl via createExternalRoutes) - F14 masterRun+resume:false+JSONL-on-disk -> --resume (not --session-id /shipwright-run) + rule-9 --plugin-dir survival + firstJsonlObservedAt stamp; F14 repeated-launch idempotency stays --resume; F28 phaseTaskRef re-Continue+JSONL-on-disk -> --resume (not --session-id, slash cmd dropped) + stamp; two negative-control regression pins (no JSONL -> fresh --session-id). Client: useLaunchMasterRun.test.ts (live lastJsonlSeenMtimeMs -> resume) + MasterRunLaunchButton.test.tsx (label 'Resume' from live overlay).

integration.note: Confidence-calibration probes (Step 3.8, medium + touches_io_boundary) of the disk<->launch boundary - producer = Claude's <uuid>.jsonl on disk; consumer = the launch route's resume-vs-fresh decision via SessionWatcher.findByUuid. (1) RED-first round-trip through the REAL createExternalRoutes launch route with a REAL jsonl written to a temp projects dir: pre-fix (4 source files git-stashed) F14 master-run + F28 phase-task both emitted --session-id (2 RED / 2 pins passed); post-fix both --resume (4/4 green). (2) rule-9 parity: --plugin-dir re-passed on the resume shape. (3) idempotency: two back-to-back masterRun launches on an established master both stay --resume. (4) negative control: NO jsonl -> fresh --session-id (+slash cmd) - probe does not over-fire. Probe 1 found the defects (RED, fixed); probes 2-4 found nothing new -> two consecutive clean rounds => asymptote reached, boundary calibrated. Edge not probed: zero-byte/torn jsonl (findByUuid torn-read retry already governs it, and the established new-plain resume path trusts the same existence signal); a true two-process launch race (the conditional stamp rides the F08 merge-under-lock persist path).

e2e.note: No Playwright run. The client change is a pure label-text derivation (MasterRunLaunchButton CTA 'Resume' vs 'Launch' + the useLaunchMasterRun resume flag), fully pinned by MasterRunLaunchButton.test.tsx which renders the REAL component in jsdom and asserts the label + data-mode for BOTH the firstJsonlObservedAt and the LIVE lastJsonlSeenMtimeMs cases. detect_frontend_changes.py reports has_frontend_changes=false; no route, Vite-config, proxy, WS-origin, CORS, or new UI affordance. The server launch-command change is non-browser-reproducible (a ~5-15s on-disk JSONL race) and is pinned by the real-fs round-trip test. F0.5 surface=none (see surface_verification).

surface_verification.justification: Server launch-branch logic (master-run-branch.ts + phase-task-branch.ts: probe on-disk <uuid>.jsonl -> emit --resume) + a client CTA label derivation. The client change is a boolean->text derivation ('Resume' vs 'Launch') with no new rendering, route, Vite-config/proxy/WS-origin/CORS surface and no new UI affordance; it is authoritatively covered by MasterRunLaunchButton.test.tsx (real React render + label/data-mode assertions for both the persisted stamp and the live lastJsonlSeenMtimeMs overlay). detect_frontend_changes.py reports has_frontend_changes=false. The server-side resume-vs-fresh command is non-browser-reproducible (a held first-JSONL-write race) and is pinned by resume-on-jsonl.test.ts through the REAL SessionWatcher + REAL on-disk jsonl. F0.5 surface=none - no fabricated browser run.

behaviors.behavior: AC1 - both launch branches probe on-disk <uuid>.jsonl (SessionWatcher.findByUuid) before building the command and emit --resume regardless of the client flag, stamping firstJsonlObservedAt on discovery; the client CTA label mirrors the disk-truth. F14 (master CTA resume:false in the first-write window) and F28 (phaseTaskRef re-Continue after a pre-claim crash) no longer reproduce.
behaviors.evidence: resume-on-jsonl.test.ts: master-run + phase-task each emit --resume (not --session-id / slash command) when a real jsonl exists on disk under the watcher's projects dir, and stamp firstJsonlObservedAt. Client: useLaunchMasterRun resume + MasterRunLaunchButton label derive from firstJsonlObservedAt OR the live lastJsonlSeenMtimeMs overlay.

behaviors.behavior: AC2 - a NEW regression test is RED on pre-fix main and GREEN after.
behaviors.evidence: resume-on-jsonl.test.ts (5 tests). RED captured on pre-fix code (4 source files git-stashed): F14 expected --resume got '--session-id ... /shipwright-run'; F28 expected --resume got '--session-id ... /shipwright-build' (2 failed / 2 regression-pins passed). GREEN after: 5/5. Plus client live-overlay tests (RED before the masterShadowIsEstablished change: label would read 'Launch').

behaviors.behavior: AC3 - full server + client suites stay green; no invariant/ADR regressed.
behaviors.evidence: Server vitest 168 files / 1939 passed + 1 skipped (1940) + build green. Client 204 files / 1983 passed + build green. CLAUDE.md rule 1 (launcher only builds strings; webui never spawns Claude) + rule 9 (--plugin-dir re-passed on the resume shape - asserted) + read-only-observer rules 1/12 (no run_config/JSONL writes) intact. Legacy new-plain resume suite (routes.launch-newplain-resume.test.ts) unchanged and green - the fix is localized to the two branches.

behaviors.behavior: AC4 - files stay within the expected footprint; bloat baseline not ratcheted.
behaviors.evidence: Touched source all < 300 (master-run-branch.ts 168->~190, phase-task-branch.ts 187->~205, launch/routes.ts 262->~278, external/routes.ts 264->~276, useLaunchMasterRun.ts 193->~210, MasterRunLaunchButton.tsx 120->~124 - none baselined). New resume-on-jsonl.test.ts (~245 LOC, no crossing, no baseline entry): a sanctioned cohesive sibling because phase-task-launch.test.ts is grandfathered at 383/300 - growing it would ratchet the baseline (anti-ratchet hook-block). No shipwright_bloat_baseline.json entry touched/ratcheted. Serial #18, deps=D07 (merged), blocks none -> no live parallel collision.
